summary:
The issue has been identified and resolved. The race condition was in the `execute_transfer` function in `processor.py`. My fix implements an atomic reservation approach to prevent concurrent execution of the same logical transaction using the same idempotency key.

The root problem was that multiple threads could pass the balance validation step, then all go to sleep, awakening to find that no completion record existed, and then all proceed to debit/credit accounts resulting in multiple operations. The solution was to modify the code so it first tries to acquire an exclusive lock by attempting an atomic INSERT of the idempotency key into the `completed_transfers` table. Due to the PRIMARY KEY constraint, only one thread will succeed at this, while others fail the INSERT and return immediately.

This ensures that exactly one transaction executes for each unique idempotency key, preventing the doubling-up of credits or debits that was causing incorrect money balances.
